records.ety: [Ac- (perh. for the article a or for L. ad) + E. complice. See Complice.]

quotes: Success unto our valiant general, And happiness to his accomplices!

defs: An associate in the commission of a crime; a participator in an offense, whether a principal or an accessory.
notes: It is followed by with or of before a person and by in (or sometimes of) before the crime; as, A was an accomplice with B in the murder of C. Dryden uses it with to before a thing. “Suspected for accomplice to the fire.” Dryden.
